sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Tweag by Modus Create's delivery of three Cardano core infrastructure work packages over 2026-2027: Peras v1, History Expiry, and Conformance Testing for Peras and Leios.
Recipient: stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr via Intersect treasury reserve contract administration

Deliverables: Peras v1 production cryptography, KillSwitch, mainnet readiness, support and maintenance, History Expiry partial-history nodes to reduce SPO storage costs, Conformance Testing framework extended for Peras and Leios, Ready-for-activation governance-dependent outputs including merged code, reproducible releases, runbooks, benchmarks, and governance-action packages, Public demos at least every two months, status updates, and community coordination channels
Deadline/expiry: 2026-2027 delivery period; exact milestone dates not stated in document

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳18,263,496.00, stated as USD $4,565,874.00, for three interdependent Cardano infrastructure work packages delivered by Tweag by Modus Create over 2026-2027.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Peras v1 is presented as enabling faster finality of about 2 minutes compared with about 12 minutes today, but remains undeployed on mainnet and needs production cryptography, operational tooling, and hard-fork readiness work.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer states that Leios-driven throughput could cause SPO disk usage to surge to about 1 GB per hour at 100-1000 TPS, making full-history requirements economically prohibitive without History Expiry.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The three work packages are described as a single delivery pipeline whose components are interdependent, with conformance testing supporting Peras validation and History Expiry reducing SPO costs.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The budget is justified using an average market rate of $176 per hour for senior Cardano infrastructure engineers and an ADA/USD conversion rate of 0.25 based on a claimed five-year average.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect is requested to administer the contract and audit process, with milestone-based fixed price contracts where scope is well-defined and a legal contract between Tweag and Cardano Development Holdings.
missing: Independent evidence for: Peras v1 is presented as enabling faster finality of about 2 minutes compared with about 12 minutes today, but remains undeployed on mainnet and needs production cryptography, operational tooling, and hard-fork readiness work.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states that Leios-driven throughput could cause SPO disk usage to surge to about 1 GB per hour at 100-1000 TPS, making full-history requirements economically prohibitive without History Expiry.

Independent evidence for: The three work packages are described as a single delivery pipeline whose components are interdependent, with conformance testing supporting Peras validation and History Expiry reducing SPO costs.

Independent evidence for: The budget is justified using an average market rate of $176 per hour for senior Cardano infrastructure engineers and an ADA/USD conversion rate of 0.25 based on a claimed five-year average.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests ₳18,263,496.00, stated as USD $4,565,874.00, for three interdependent Cardano infrastructure work packages delivered by Tweag by Modus Create over 2026-2027." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Peras v1 is presented as enabling faster finality of about 2 minutes compared with about 12 minutes today, but remains undeployed on mainnet and needs production cryptography, operational tooling, and hard-fork readiness work." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
